title: picea engelmannii subsp. mexicana
intro: Picea engelmannii subsp. mexicana, also known as Engelmann spruce, is a species of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and the southwestern United States. The essential oil extracted from its needles is used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and treating wounds and skin irritations due to its antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Picea engelmannii subsp. mexicana include essential oils, extracts, and teas, which can be used topically or ingested under medical supervision.
